content: Warren's SQLite timestamps are stored as ISO8601 TEXT (e.g., agents.registered_at, runs.started_at, events.ts), not drizzle's integer mode. Decided per SPEC §9 sketch — events.ts is 'ISO8601 from burrow envelope', so we don't translate at the stream boundary. Burrow uses integer mode timestamps; warren deliberately diverges. When adding new tables, follow this pattern: text("col_at").notNull() with toISOString() at the call site.

runs.id (warren's run_ id, generated by core/ids.ts) and runs.burrow_run_id (burrow's run_ id, returned by POST /burrows/:id/runs) share the same prefix shape but are independent identities. SPEC §9 calls this out: 'id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, -- run_xxx (warren's, not burrow's)'. When wiring callers, never confuse them: warren run id is what the UI/HTTP API speaks; burrow run id is what we send to burrow's stream/cancel/inbox endpoints.

title: Inline state-machine in repos/runs.ts
rationale: Warren currently has only one state machine (runs: queued/running/succeeded/failed/cancelled), so ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS + assertRunTransition live in src/db/repos/runs.ts rather than a separate core/state-machine.ts (the pattern burrow uses with two state machines). Extract to core/state-machine.ts when a second state machine appears (e.g., projects clone/active/archived, schedules enabled/disabled in V2).

name: drizzle migration filename pinning
description: drizzle-kit generate emits 0000_<random>.sql by default. We rename to 0000_init.sql and update src/db/migrations/meta/_journal.json's 'tag' field to match ("tag": "0000_init"). The migrate runner reads the filename via the journal, so both must agree. Repeat the rename + journal edit for every future migration; pick a stable, descriptive tag (0001_add_schedules, etc.). Burrow uses the same convention.

Migration 0003 (orphan_runs_on_project_delete) used the canonical SQLite '12-step ALTER' pattern with PRAGMA foreign_keys=OFF/ON inside the migration body. drizzle-orm wraps each migration in BEGIN/COMMIT, and SQLite silently ignores PRAGMA foreign_keys toggled inside a multi-statement transaction. With FK still ON, DROP TABLE runs failed against any production DB that had events rows referencing runs (SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_FOREIGNKEY at migrator.js).
resolution: Hoist the pragma out of the migration: src/db/client.ts sets PRAGMA foreign_keys=OFF on the raw connection BEFORE migrate() opens its transaction, then PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON after migrate() returns. The migration's own PRAGMA lines stay (drizzle-kit auto-generates them; harmless no-ops). Added regression test in src/db/client.test.ts that builds a partial migrations folder (0000-0002), populates events with FK-referencing rows, then re-opens with the full folder — the test reproduces the SQLite error without the fix.

content: PRAGMA foreign_keys cannot be toggled inside a transaction (SQLite silently ignores it). Drizzle's migrate() runs each migration body inside BEGIN/COMMIT, so any FK-disabling pragma needed for the SQLite '12-step ALTER' rebuild pattern (CREATE __new / INSERT SELECT / DROP / RENAME) MUST be set on the raw connection before migrate() runs, not inside the .sql file. src/db/client.ts owns that toggle: FK=OFF before migrate, FK=ON after.

RUN_FAILURE_REASONS (and similar drizzle enum unions) is a TS-only narrowing — text({ enum }) does not generate a SQL CHECK constraint, so adding a new enum value is a one-line schema.ts edit with no migration needed. The SQL column stays plain text.

runs.pr_url (warren-f6af, migration 0004_add_run_pr_url) is a nullable text column reap fills in via repos.runs.setPrUrl(...) before the terminal transition. Persisted independently from finalize() so the URL also lands on the reap.completed event payload.

triggers table PK is a composite string '<projectId>:<triggerId>' (single text column, not a multi-column PK) — the trigger's authoring identity in .warren/triggers.yaml is the durable handle, so the scheduler tick writes back state without juggling a generated id. Use makeTriggerRowId(projectId, triggerId) from src/db/schema.ts to build the key consistently.

TriggersRepo.upsert uses undefined-vs-null semantics on patch fields: omitted (undefined) preserves the existing column, explicit null clears it. Same shape as RunsRepo.attachBurrow. The scheduler can write a fresh nextFireAt across many ticks without disturbing the lastFiredAt/lastRunId stamped at fire time.

warren workers table (src/db/schema.ts, warren-b0a3) holds {name PK, url, state, addedAt} with state enum WORKER_STATES = ['healthy','draining','unreachable'] (default 'healthy'); bearer token is NOT a column — single shared BURROW_API_TOKEN env across the pool (plan pl-9ba1 alternative #3). Zero-row table is the steady state for single-worker deploys; BurrowClientPool synthesizes a local row from WARREN_BURROW_* env vars when empty.

WorkersRepo.upsert (src/db/repos/workers.ts, warren-b0a3) preserves an existing row's state when 'state' is omitted from the patch — config reloads (step 7 [workers] block) must NOT clobber probe/drain-derived liveness. Pass state explicitly to override. Also preserves addedAt across re-registration; url updates in place.

warren burrows table (src/db/schema.ts, warren-135b) is the warren-side {burrow_id → worker_id} placement registry. (id PK, worker_id text NOT NULL, added_at) with index on worker_id. worker_id is plain text — no FK to workers.name — because the zero-config single-worker deploy uses a synthetic local worker not materialized in the workers table. BurrowsRepo.create has no upsert; collisions throw (one burrow id per provisionBurrow call by construction). Source of truth for placeForBurrow sticky-by-burrow lookups.

runs.worker_id (src/db/schema.ts, warren-135b) is the denormalized copy of burrows.worker_id written at run-create time so streaming / cancel / steer paths route to the owning worker without joining burrows. Nullable for back-compat with rows written before pl-9ba1 step 4 wired this in. Composite index runs_worker_state_idx covers least-loaded queries (RunsRepo.countInflightByWorker). attachBurrow accepts workerId in its partial-patch shape alongside burrowId / burrowRunId.
